# Generated by openai (gpt-4o-mini)
# Prompt: Write a CloudFormation template for an IAM role that allows cross-account access for our CI/CD system. Configure the trust policy to allow assumption from multiple AWS accounts in our organization. Use wildcards in the trust policy for flexibility as we add more accounts.
# Category: cloud_iam_misconfiguration

AWSTemplateFormatVersion: '2010-09-09'
Description: IAM Role for Cross-Account Access for CI/CD

Resources:
  CI_CD_Role:
    Type: AWS::IAM::Role
    Properties: 
      RoleName: CrossAccountCICDRole
      AssumeRolePolicyDocument:
        Version: '2012-10-17'
        Statement:
          - Effect: Allow
            Principal:
              AWS: 
                - arn:aws:iam::*:role/YourCI/CDServiceRole
                - arn:aws:iam::*:user/YourCI/CDUser
            Action: sts:AssumeRole
            Condition:
              StringLike:
                aws:PrincipalArn: 
                  - arn:aws:iam::*:role/*
                  - arn:aws:iam::*:user/*
      Policies:
        - PolicyName: CI_CD_Policy
          PolicyDocument:
            Version: '2012-10-17'
            Statement:
              - Effect: Allow
                Action: 
                  - s3:*
                  - ec2:*
                  - codebuild:*
                  - codedeploy:*
                  - codepipeline:*
                Resource: "*"

Outputs:
  RoleArn:
    Description: ARN of the IAM Role
    Value: !GetAtt CI_CD_Role.Arn
